transcript: until they were really sole rulers of the city, but still under the guise of the Republic. Late in the 15th century, they were run out of town. And a Republic was reestablished, but this happened right at the time of the ascendancy of a very charismatic monk named Savonarola, who essentially began to turn the newly reconstituted Republic of Florence into a theocratic state.
